description:
Display a graph to the user in a dedicated Locus graph window. Use this when a graph is the clearest representation of nodes, ports, parameters, and links, such as shader networks, animation controllers, state machines, behavior trees, dependency graphs, or Blueprint-style flows.

The agent submits node definitions and links. Locus renders the graph with built-in styling, port connection UI, parameter controls, selectable layout modes, selectable node styles, and optional directed link arrows. Use layout.nodeStyle=state for compact Unreal/Unity-style state machine nodes. Use layout.statePortPlacement=vertical for top/bottom state-node ports. Use layout.directed=true for directed links, or link.directed for per-link overrides. Use layout.mode=manual when you want to supply and preserve explicit node x/y coordinates.

Use editable=false when the graph is for inspection only. The window opens and the tool returns immediately so work can continue.

Use editable=true when the user must review or edit the graph before the agent proceeds. The tool waits until the user confirms an option in the graph window, then returns the selected option and the modified graph data. Closing the window cancels the wait.

Options work like ask_user_question choices: provide up to 3 concise labels with descriptions. They are shown as confirmation actions in editable mode.

editable.description: Whether the user can edit the graph and the tool must wait for confirmation. Defaults to false.

returnImage.description: When editable=true and layout.mode=manual, attach a PNG preview of the submitted layout. Use only when the current endpoint supports image understanding.

nodes.description: Graph nodes. Coordinates are optional unless layout.mode=manual is used to preserve an agent-authored layout.

type.description: Optional node type used for display and downstream processing.

nodeStyle.description: Optional per-node visual style. Use state for compact Unreal/Unity-style state machine nodes.

parameters.description: Editable node parameters shown inside the node.

type.description: string, text, number, boolean, select, or color.

options.description: Select options when type is select.

data.description: Optional agent-defined payload kept with the node.

links.description: Links between nodes or ports. Use from.portId and to.portId for port-level links.

directed.description: Optional per-link arrow override. Defaults to layout.directed.

data.description: Optional agent-defined payload kept with the link.

layout.description: Optional layout settings. Defaults to automatic layout for missing coordinates.

auto.description: true, false, missing, always, or off.

mode.description: Layout mode. flow is a directed process layout. dependency is a network layout for asset/code dependencies. state is a force layout for cyclic state machines. radial places related nodes around roots. manual preserves supplied node x/y coordinates and fills only missing positions.

nodeStyle.description: Node visual style. blueprint shows ports and parameters like a node editor. state uses compact Unreal/Unity-style state machine nodes.

directed.description: Show arrowheads on links by default. link.directed can override this per link.

statePortPlacement.description: Port placement for state-style nodes. auto follows layout.direction; vertical uses top input and bottom output for downward layouts.

label.description: Short display text for this option.

description.description: Brief explanation of what this option means.

value.description: Optional stable value returned to the agent.

graph.description: Optional full GraphData object. If present, it is used instead of top-level nodes and links.
